reasoning: The January 2025 AI Diffusion Rule establishing export controls was formally rescinded by the new administration in May 2025, indicating a strong shift toward deregulation. The administration replaced strict global licensing requirements with 'heightened global due diligence requirements' and self-policing. The 'Chip Security Act', which proposes tracking mechanisms on chips, was introduced in Congress in May 2025 but has not been enacted, and even if it passed late in the year, its built-in 180-day implementation delay pushes the effective date well into 2026, missing the December 31, 2025 deadline. A search of the Federal Register up to October 2025 yields no new final rules matching the resolution criteria. Thus, a YES resolution is highly improbable.
